patch.value: ADR: SDK define* builders are zero-cost identity helpers (defineConfig pattern), generic for object defs / non-generic for function defs

CONTEXT: Adding authoring-time define* builders (pm-12tj) for every extension registration surface raises two design questions. DECISION 1 - pure identity vs validating: define* builders are zero-cost identity functions (return the argument unchanged), exactly like the existing defineExtension and the Vite/Vue defineConfig/defineComponent ecosystem convention. Validation stays in the runtime register*/loader and the test-time assert* helpers; duplicating it in define* would make the cost non-obvious, diverge from defineExtension, and create two sources of truth. DECISION 2 - generic vs non-generic signature: object-definition builders use a generic constraint `<T extends Def>(x: T): T` to PRESERVE literal types (matches defineExtension) while still type-checking against the contract; function-definition builders use the non-generic `(fn: FnType): FnType` because a generic constrained to a whole function type SUPPRESSES contextual parameter typing for bare arrows in .js packages (the param would fall back to implicit any) - the non-generic form lets TypeScript contextually type the handler parameter from the declared FnType. CONSEQUENCES: package authors get type-checking + handler-param inference in plain-JS packages without TS annotation syntax; the builders are tree-shakeable; the author->register->test ergonomics surface is symmetric and complete.

author: codex-implementation-agent
message: Continuing SDK authoring-builder ADR closeout by aligning package scaffold guidance with define* best-practice without creating duplicate lineage.

Package authoring docs explain define* builders and scaffold upgrade path

value.author: codex-implementation-agent
value.text: Evidence: duplicate-safe all-status search found pm-12tj as the closed implementation and pm-3mph as the open ADR; no open GitHub PRs or issues; Dependabot alerts empty; pnpm audit found no known vulnerabilities; pnpm outdated only reports Node-major-bound @types/node 26 and commander 15 while current wanted versions remain pinned. Implemented package scaffold README guidance for the SDK define* upgrade path after peer dependencies are installed, while preserving import-free entrypoints for local install-before-npm-install. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-scaffold-define-guidance.spec.ts; pm test pm-3mph --run --progress; pnpm build; pnpm quality:static; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage = 100/100/100/100 (245 files, 4853 tests); pnpm security:scan; pnpm contracts:check; pm package doctor --project --detail deep --json; pm health --check-only --summary --json; manual temp-dir smoke at /tmp/tmp.DDR3X0sX97 with pm init, pm package init --capability hooks, local install before dependencies, command invocation, README define* check, npm install --no-save current checkout, and npm test.

patch.value: Closed the ADR by aligning generated package authoring guidance with the shipped zero-cost define* SDK builder decision while keeping the runtime scaffold dependency-light.
Package authors can see when and how to use define* builders from generated package scaffolds, without breaking the local install-before-dependencies workflow.

Generated package README now includes a defineCommand/defineAfterCommandHook upgrade path; standalone extension scaffolds remain import-free; focused regression test, build, static/docstring gate, full coverage, package doctor, security, contracts, health, changelog, and temp-dir smoke passed.
ADR closeout complete: generated package scaffolds now document the define* builder upgrade path after peer SDK install, docs align, regression coverage added, and full verification passed.
